2016-10-25.abstract: Miaomiao Lake Village is just one of the new communities built to accommodate the world’s largest environmental migration project. But residents are struggling to adapt.

2016-10-25.abstract: The four adults died when a raft in a white-water attraction flipped over, trapping them in the ride’s machinery, the authorities said.

2016-10-26.title: Afghan Vice President Hints That Turmoil Awaits if He Is Not Respected
2016-10-26.abstract: Gen. Abdul Rashid Dostum appears to have issued a veiled threat of turning on his own government, which he said suppresses him and his Uzbek constituency.

2016-10-26.title: North Korea Giving Up Nuclear Arms ‘a Lost Cause,’ Official Says
2016-10-26.abstract: At the Council on Foreign Relations, James R. Clapper Jr. also said he “wouldn’t put it past” Russia to shoot down an American plane in Syria if a no-fly zone were imposed.

2016-10-26.abstract: The dwindling power of the opposition-led National Assembly means the decision to bring political charges will likely have no practical effect.

2016-10-26.abstract: Members of an elite force from the 1948 war are protesting a decision to memorialize a general who has been posthumously accused of sexual assault and criminal ties.

2016-10-26.abstract: Participants of the mock funeral program write a testament, don a shroud, and climb in a coffin, which is then hammered shut for 10 long minutes.

2016-10-27.abstract: The men were arrested on Tuesday, transferred to the country’s main counterterrorism center in Tunis, the capital, and then apparently handed over to American officials.

2016-10-27.title: 8 Nursing Home Killings Leave Ontario Asking: Why?
2016-10-27.abstract: New details emerged about a nurse charged with eight counts of murder over a seven-year period, but a possible motive for the killings remained unclear.

2016-10-27.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of protesters in Caracas demanded a recall referendum that would expel the president from office.

2016-10-27.abstract: Mr. Batlle was the first Latin American leader to propose the partial legalization of drugs as a way to reduce trafficking, money laundering and profiteering by cartels.

2016-10-27.abstract: The assault in Idlib Province may have been the deadliest attack on a school since the war began in 2011, Unicef said.

2016-10-28.abstract: Adding to tensions between Russia and the West, Russia’s United Nations ambassador said a report that Syrian forces had used chemical weapons was unconvincing.

2016-10-28.title: Coast of Antarctica Will Host World’s Largest Marine Reserve
2016-10-28.abstract: Representatives from 24 nations agreed to create a zone that will encompass 600,000 square miles of ocean in the Ross Sea in Antarctica.

2016-10-28.abstract: Employees at an air-monitoring station in Xi’an, in northern China, stuffed gauze into the pollution monitors so that the air would seem cleaner, a report said.

2016-10-28.abstract: The veteran democracy campaigner Hu Ping says that Chinese concepts of freedom are not imports from the West, but a response to oppression at home.

2016-10-28.title: Brazil’s Mythical ‘Super-Chicken’: What, Exactly, Is a Chester?
2016-10-28.abstract: It is a dinner staple in Brazil at Christmastime, but few have seen a Chester alive. The mystery has allowed fanciful stories to swirl, to the delight of the company that sells the bird.

2016-10-29.title: In Myanmar, Military Action Forces Some to Flee: ‘We Just Had to Run Away to Save Our Lives’
2016-10-29.abstract: Ko Thu Ya, a member of the Rohingya minority, felt forced to leave his home with his family amid a military lockdown in the western part of the country.

2016-10-29.abstract: Distinctive vehicles, once a symbol of national hopes, carry besieged rebels and civilians toward an uncertain fate as they yield land to government forces.

2016-10-29.abstract: Greece’s creditors have pressed to allow debtors’ properties to be seized and auctioned. Though officials insist homes are safe, owners are panicking.

2016-10-30.abstract: The temblor, with a reported magnitude of 6.5, caused fresh damage to towns hit by recent quakes, but few injuries were reported.

2016-10-30.abstract: Many prisoners were said to be killed in an attack by coalition jets on a security complex in Al Hudaydah, on the Red Sea coast.

2016-10-30.abstract: The title, previously given only to the leaders Mao Zedong, Deng Xiaoping and Jiang Zemin, could reinforce Mr. Xi’s authority to push through policies.

2016-10-31.title: 300 Million Children Breathe Highly Toxic Air, Unicef Reports
2016-10-31.abstract: The vast majority, about 220 million, live in South Asia, in places where air pollution is at least six times the level considered safe, the United Nations Children’s Fund said.

2016-10-31.abstract: Mr. Shavit, known for his a deeply introspective look at Israel’s remarkable rise, said he was “ashamed” of his actions toward women.

2016-10-31.abstract: The candidate, Igor Dodon, finished far ahead of pro-Western challengers, but failed to win the outright majority needed to avoid a runoff election next month.

2016-10-31.title: Making Online Literature Pay Big in China
2016-10-31.abstract: Zhang Wei, China’s top-earning digital novelist, talks about the benefits of online publishing and his dream of creating a Disney-style empire.

2016-10-31.title: Australia’s Proposed Lifetime Ban on Boat-Borne Refugees Draws Fire
2016-10-31.abstract: The ban would apply to adult asylum seekers who try to come to Australia by boat, regardless of where they settle and are eventually granted citizenship.
